channel.title: Defense
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: Hegseth orders withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from Germany
item.description: The drawdown, which comes after the country’s leader sparred with President Donald Trump over the Iran war, leaves about 33,000 troops there.

media:content.media:title: Members of the U.S. Army 3rd Brigade, 1st Cavalry Division, participate in the "Combined Resolve" military exercise at the U.S. Army 7th Army Training Command Joint Multinational Readiness Center in Hohenfels, Germany, on April 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Aaron Pellish and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Aaron Pellish and Paul McLeary

item.title: Trump tells Congress the Iran war has ‘terminated’ as legal deadline hits
item.description: The letter to lawmakers attempts to justify why the president is not seeking congressional authorization after the conflict reaches a 60-day threshold.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump speaks in the Oval Office on April 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Eli Stokols
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Eli Stokols

item.title: Hegseth says Iran war can continue despite legal deadline
item.description: The Pentagon chief told senators a ceasefire in the Middle East pauses a 60-day war powers clock.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, left, and Chair of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Dan Caine testify before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Capitol Hill on April 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien

item.title: Trump’s call to reduce US troops in Germany shocks Pentagon
item.description: The Defense Department "was not expecting it," a congressional aide said.

media:content.media:title: U.S. Army troops simulate an attack against an enemy during a demonstration of capabilities at the Grafenwoehr training grounds on March 13, 2026, near Grafenwoehr, Germany.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, Paul McLeary and Stefanie Bolzen
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, Paul McLeary and Stefanie Bolzen

item.title: Trump says US considering reducing troops in Germany
item.description: There are 38,000 U.S. troops and personnel stationed in Germany, and cutting forces there would face opposition on Capitol Hill.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump speaks during a meeting with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz in the Oval Office of the White House in Washington, March 3, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch and Aaron Pellish
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch and Aaron Pellish

item.title: As Iran war drags on, Hegseth labels Dems obstacle to peace
item.description: His decision to label critics as short-sighted and unpatriotic pulls a page from the Donald Trump playbook: when threatened, attack.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testifies before the House Armed Services Committee on Capitol Hill in Washington, on April 29, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien

item.title: Pentagon official: Iran war has cost $25B
item.description: The figure is the most specific price tag the administration has provided on the U.S. military conflict.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chair Gen. Dan Caine testify before the House Armed Services Committee on April 29, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Leo Shane III

item.title: Senate Armed Services chair: Trump should resume strikes on Iran
item.description: Sen. Roger Wicker said the administration needs to “finish destroying Iran’s conventional military capabilities.”

media:content.media:title: Sen. Roger Wicker, R-Miss., the Senate Armed Services Committee chair, leaves a closed-door briefing on the Iran War at the Capitol on March 10, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III

item.title: Hegseth declares victory while preparing for more war with Iran
item.description: The administration is still sending more forces to the region — and asking for help from allies — even as the White House says the conflict is over.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks to members of the media during a press briefing at the Pentagon on April 16, 2026, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary

item.title: Golden Dome dreams face harsh budget reality
item.description: Congressional Republicans are increasingly reluctant to move ahead with a partisan budget bill that was supposed to cover the program’s costs.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump and Rep. Jim Banks (R-Ind.) listen as Space Force Gen. Michael Guetlein speaks in the Oval Office about the Golden Dome missile defense system May 20, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Audrey Decker and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Audrey Decker and Paul McLeary

item.title: Acting Navy secretary, once a political lightning rod, finds surprising support
item.description: Hung Cao, known as a partisan firebrand, has built credibility inside the Pentagon.

media:content.media:title: Then-Republican Senate candidate Hung Cao speaks during a rally for President Donald Trump on June 28, 2024, in Chesapeake, Virginia.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, Dasha Burns, Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, Dasha Burns, Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien

item.title: Senate again declines to curb Trump on Iran amid uncertain ceasefire, peace talks
item.description: It's the fifth time a Democratic-led effort to rein in Trump has failed, although Republicans are eyeing an approaching war powers deadline.

media:content.media:title: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.), Sen. Tim Kaine (D-Va.), Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.), and Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.) speak with veterans about the Iran war at Capitol Hill on March 18, 2026 in Washington, D.C.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: Navy secretary is out amid Pentagon infighting
item.description: John Phelan sparked tensions within the department over his support for a new battleship.

media:content.media:title: John Phelan, who served just over a year in his post, had helped conceive of the new battleships to curry favor with President Donald Trump, according to two people familiar with the matter.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, Paul McLeary, Daniel Lippman and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, Paul McLeary, Daniel Lippman and Connor O'Brien

item.title: Trump weighs consequences for NATO allies on ‘naughty’ list
item.description: The effort is the latest sign President Donald Trump plans to make good on his threats against members not deemed “model allies."

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Claudine Hellmuth/POLITICO (source images via Getty)
media:content.media:title: Main.NATO-Naughty-Nice.jpg

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch and Paul McLeary

item.title: Iran talks on hold over Trump's blockade
item.description: Vice President JD Vance, who'd planned to fly to Islamabad, remained in Washington over the impasse.

media:content.media:title: A boat sails past a tanker anchored on the Strait of Hormuz off the coast Qeshm island, Iran, Saturday, April 18, 2026. (AP Photo/Asghar Besharati)

item.dc:creator: By Eli Stokols, Felicia Schwartz and Scott Waldman
item.dc:contributor: By Eli Stokols, Felicia Schwartz and Scott Waldman

item.title: A depleted Space Force races to prepare for Trump’s spending spree
item.description: The six-year-old service is hiring like "there’s never been a tomorrow" to execute a potential budget boon.

media:content.media:title: A solider wears a U.S. Space Force uniform during a ceremony for U.S. Air Force airmen transitioning to U.S. Space Force guardian designations at Travis Air Force Base, California, on Feb. 12, 2021.

item.dc:creator: By Audrey Decker
item.dc:contributor: By Audrey Decker

item.title: ‘More compromises’: Trump wants an end to Iran war
item.description: While the president claims he's got the cards, he may have to give ground to get a deal.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump speaks with reporters before departing on Marine One from the South Lawn of the White House, Thursday, April 16, 2026, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Eli Stokols and Phelim Kine
item.dc:contributor: By Eli Stokols and Phelim Kine

item.title: Pentagon leaders 'get to pick the leaders that they want,' Army secretary says while praising ousted top general
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties expressed unease with the sudden departure of Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George.

media:content.media:title: U.S. Secretary of the Army Daniel Driscoll (left) fist bumps Chief of Staff of the Army Gen. Randy George before testifying during a Senate Committee on Armed Services at the Dirksen Senate Building, June 5, 2025, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: House narrowly rejects limits on Trump as Iran war drags on
item.description: Republican leaders kept defections to a minimum, signaling the party isn’t ready to publicly break with the White House on the war effort just yet.

media:content.media:title: House Foreign Affairs Committee ranking Democrat Gregory Meeks forced the second vote to halt the U.S. military campaign against Iran since the conflict began.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: Navy plays deadly game of hide-and-seek with Iranian mines
item.description: As more American warships head to the Middle East, the mine-hunting mission might be the next big job.

media:content.media:credit: Mass Communication Specialist 3rd Class Riley Gasdia/U.S. Navy
media:content.media:title: More than a dozen American warships are already in the region, and more are on the way.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary

item.title: Republicans reject Iran limits as war powers milestone nears
item.description: The Middle East conflict faces a ticking war powers clock for lawmakers to authorize continued operations that could be a bigger political headache for Trump and Republicans.

media:content.media:title: Sen. Tammy Duckworth led Democrats in a fourth unsuccessful vote to halt the U.S. military campaign against Iran.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: Hegseth again skipping Ukraine meeting, will send top lieutenant instead
item.description: As the U.S. continues to look away from Europe, fewer top officials are visiting longtime allies.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks to members of the media during a press briefing at the Pentagon in Washington, April 8, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary

item.title: Allies try to puzzle out US blockade of Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump is trying to ramp up the pressure on Tehran — but allies and officials are scrambling to understand how the blockade will help.

media:content.media:credit: Mass Communication Specialist 3rd Class Zachary Pearson/U.S. Navy via AP
media:content.media:title: In this Nov. 19, 2019, photo made available by the U.S. Navy, the aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln, left, the air-defense destroyer HMS Defender and the guided-missile destroyer USS Farragut transit the Strait of Hormuz with the guided-missile cruiser USS Leyte Gulf.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary, Jack Detsch and Phelim Kine
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary, Jack Detsch and Phelim Kine

item.title: What Trump’s blowup with Rutte was really about
item.description: The president’s tirade against NATO chief Mark Rutte stemmed from his frustration that allies wouldn’t let the U.S. access military bases for attacks against Iran.

media:content.media:title: Secretary General Mark Rutte’s visit might actually have been well-timed.

item.dc:creator: By Eli Stokols, Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch
item.dc:contributor: By Eli Stokols, Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch

item.title: NATO chief insists Europe is ‘scrambling’ to help with Iran war after latest Trump attacks
item.description: The head of the alliance tried to thread the needle between an irate U.S. president and NATO members who feel unfairly maligned.

media:content.media:title: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte speaks at the Ronald Reagan Institute on April 9, 2026, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary

item.title: ‘Seems like losing’: What the US hasn’t won in Iran
item.description: As negotiations begin this weekend in Pakistan, the war has reaffirmed Iran’s regional significance.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth looks toward Joint Chiefs of Staff chair Gen. Dan Caine, as they speak to the media at the Pentagon on April 8, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch

item.title: Hegseth declares victory in Iran but says US forces will remain in region
item.description: The Defense Secretary repeatedly referred to the U.S. military operation in the past tense.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks to members of the media during a press briefing at the Pentagon in Washington, Wednesday, April 8, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III

item.title: Pentagon’s new plans in Iran give Trump a way out of war crime accusations
item.description: Defense officials are expanding a list of strike options.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks with reporters during a news conference with President Donald Trump and Joint Chiefs of Staff Chair Gen. Dan Caine at the White House on April 6, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary and Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary and Leo Shane III

item.title: NATO’s Rutte to meet with Trump at White House amid attacks on alliance
item.description: The president has grown increasingly furious with the decades-old alliance amid the war in Iran.

media:content.media:title: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte gives a press conference about NATO's general annual report in Brussels on March 26, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Aaron Pellish
item.dc:contributor: By Aaron Pellish

item.title: One pilot rescued after fighter jet crash in Iran
item.description: The second crew member’s fate is unknown.

media:content.media:title: An F-15 Eagle fighter aircraft flies on Sept. 24, 2024.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch and Paul McLeary
